response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced polyglot engineer with genuine systems-programming depth (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second) and a strong testing and knowledge-sharing culture. However, the role's minimum qualifications center on 8 years of C++, which the resume does not support — C++ appears only in a short National Instruments stint and a thesis project, with the career built on DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js. Formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps teams and sustained large-scale cloud compute platform ownership are also not evidenced, making the fit for a C++-centric cloud platform Technical Lead weak despite adjacent skills.
strengths: Deep low-latency distributed-systems experience (microservices at millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, and delivered training/workshops
Broad full-stack and infrastructure breadth including Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, and open-source platform ownership
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience; primary systems language is DLang
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers or leading cross-team technical strategy
Limited production-scale cloud compute platform architecture experience; resume timeline also contains inconsistent, overlapping dates

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical leadership experience
